title: From Hallucination to Grounding: Diagnosing Visual Spatial Intelligence via CRISP
abstract: arXiv:2606.26535v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Current VLM evaluations often conflate language priors with genuine spatial reasoning. To address this, we introduce CRISP, a novel structural-diagnostic evaluation paradigm that assesses visual spatial intelligence through consistency, the alignment between implicit perception and explicit reasoning. Unlike traditional black-box QA, CRISP utilizes metric 3D Scene Graphs and an oracle intervention protocol to decouple latent reasoning capabilities from perceptual bottlenecks. This granular diagnosis uncovers a systematic perception-reasoning disconnect. Crucially, we reveal that while proprietary models possess robust latent reasoning engines, they suffer from inaccurate metric estimation and a critical failure to leverage their implicit structural representations. Conversely, open-source models remain fundamentally bottlenecked by their lack of multi-hop compositional reasoning. By shifting the focus from merely ``guessing correctly''

Title: From Hallucination to Grounding: Diagnosing Visual Spatial Intelligence via CRISP
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26535v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Current VLM evaluations often conflate language priors with genuine spatial reasoning. To address this, we introduce CRISP, a novel structural-diagnostic evaluation paradigm that assesses visual spatial intelligence through consistency, the alignment between implicit perception and explicit reasoning. translated_title: 从幻觉到扎根：通过CRISP诊断视觉空间智能
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26535v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：当前的视觉语言模型（VLM）评估常常将语言先验知识与真正的空间推理混为一谈。为了解决这一问题，我们引入了CRISP，一种新颖的结构性诊断评估范式，通过一致性（即内隐感知与外显推理之间的对齐）来评估视觉空间智能。与传统的黑箱问答不同，CRISP利用度量3D场景图和一个神谕干预协议，将潜在的推理能力与感知瓶颈解耦。这种细粒度诊断揭示了系统性的感知-推理脱节。关键在于，我们发现虽然专有模型拥有强大的潜在推理引擎，但它们存在度量估计不准确的问题，并且未能利用其内隐的结构化表示。相反，开源模型则主要受限于其缺乏多跳组合推理能力。通过将关注点从仅仅“猜对”转移开。
tldr: 本文提出CRISP评估范式，通过解耦感知与推理能力，揭示了专有模型虽有强大推理引擎但存在度量估计不准和无法利用内隐结构的问题，而开源模型则受限于缺乏多跳组合推理。
en_tdlr: This paper introduces CRISP, a diagnostic paradigm that decouples perception from reasoning, revealing that proprietary models suffer from inaccurate metric estimation and failure to leverage implicit structures despite strong reasoning engines, while open-source models are bottlenecked by a lack of multi-hop compositional reasoning.
